title: rourea induta var. induta
intro: Rourea induta var. induta, also known as the "forest tree" or "wild peach", is a species of flowering plant in the family Connaraceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rourea induta var. induta include the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. In traditional herbal medicine, Rourea induta var. induta is prepared in various forms, such as tea, powder, or ointment, and is often used to aid in wound healing and to reduce symptoms of colds and flu.
